behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is in deterministic key-derivation logic, not module handoff. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: no OS/network/subprocess needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate checks: Unit setup: call keygen twice with same normalized params and once with changed param order/value; assertion: first two keys equal, changed input yields different key; catches canonicalization/collision bugs. Adjacent Integration setup: run through cache service API; assertion same; catches little beyond Unit for this behavior. Rejection-gate: behavior text only says key generation from query parameters, with no DB/network/LLM dependency, so higher levels are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Property-style unit test for key generator: verify determinism for equivalent parameter maps and non-collision for a generated corpus of distinct parameter combinations; fail on any duplicate key among distinct canonical inputs.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is rule-mapping computation (category -> TTL). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required if config is provided in-memory. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate checks: Unit setup: pass config map and categories to TTL calculator; assertion: exact durations returned; catches wrong mapping/default precedence. Adjacent Integration setup: load config through config module + service; assertion same; adds wiring risk but not required to catch stated failure mode. Rejection-gate: behavior text focuses on computed TTL by configured rule, not config I/O source or runtime infra.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test matrix over categories and defaults: assert TTL equals configured duration, including boundary categories and unknown-category fallback.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure can occur at module boundary between memory-pressure signal, config threshold, and strategy selector state. Unit on pure selector logic may miss miswiring/event timing. REAL-INFRA: can be reproduced in-process with injected memory metrics; no mandatory OS/subprocess behavior. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate checks: Integration setup: wire cache manager + metrics provider + config; drive usage below/above threshold; assertion: active policy transitions LRU->LFU at threshold crossing; catches stale config subscription/handoff bugs. Adjacent Unit setup: call decideStrategy(usage, threshold); assertion policy choice; catches formula bugs only. Rejection-gate for System: behavior text says switch based on configured threshold, not real OS memory APIs, so System is not required.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test with real cache-manager modules and fake in-process metrics source: simulate memory usage trajectory crossing threshold and assert policy switch occurs exactly once at crossing and stays consistent on subsequent operations.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this failure often appears only with real DB write semantics (transaction commit timing, trigger/listener payload shape, CDC delivery). In-process mocks can falsely pass. REAL-INFRA: requires real PostgreSQL behavior and usually real process/network boundary. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate checks: System setup: run real Postgres + app cache invalidation path, seed cache for rows, execute SQL UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE in transaction; assertion: matching keys removed after commit, non-matching keys retained; catches trigger/listener/serialization mismatch. Adjacent Integration setup: fake repository emits change events; assertion invalidation works; catches mapping logic but misses DB-originated event failures. Rejection-gate for Agentic/Workflow: behavior text is deterministic DB-cache invalidation with no AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: System test against real PostgreSQL instance: perform row writes and verify cache invalidation reflects committed row identities, including multi-row updates and rollback case (no invalidation on rollback).

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on interaction between access-tracking counters/timestamps and eviction execution in cache store. Testing policy function alone can miss integration bugs (tracker not updated, wrong key passed to remover). REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network needed. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Candidate checks: Integration setup: real cache store + tracker + selected policy, fill to capacity, perform access pattern, add new item; assertion: evicted key matches expected LRU/LFU victim; catches bookkeeping/handoff bugs. Adjacent Unit setup: test standalone victim-selection algorithm with synthetic metadata; assertion chosen victim; catches ranking logic only. Rejection-gate for System: behavior text is internal cache-full eviction logic without real subprocess/network dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Integration tests for both modes (LRU and LFU) using the actual in-memory cache modules: craft distinct recency/frequency patterns, trigger overflow, and assert exact victim key each time.
